pages.raw_text:
The Washington Water Power com
pany contemplates building an office
building on Flume street on the small
strip of ground west of Post street,
east of Lincoln street and north of
Main avenue.


j. j. Browne at one time dedicated
this strip Of ground to the city but
now claims it as his personal property
on the ground that it was never accept
ed. If the Washington Water Power
company can get possession of the
property either from the city or Mr..
Browne it is expected the building
will be commenced this summer.


Architect Albert Held has been re-
tanned by the company to draw up the
plans and can have them completed as
soon as an agreement is reached be-
tween the disputants. 1t is the plan
of the company to make the building
three-story brick structure, which
will be occupied exclusively by the
offices of the company.
